name: ds-label
description: Text label for a form control. Associates via the standard `for` attribute and can render a required marker; carries no interaction of its own.

properties.name: for
properties.description: Id of the control this label describes.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Renders a required marker after the label text.

constraints.rationale: No primitive references, no raw values.
Label text must clear AA contrast against the surface in both context token resolutions; a label is never a focus stop, so no positive tabindex is ever legitimate.
Adaptive class: API drift is structural drift.

provenance.author: ai
provenance.justification: Design decision: ds-label is a pure text/association primitive with a boolean `required` marker rather than a full validation-state API (e.g. mirroring ds-text-input's `invalid`). Rejected alternative: giving the label its own `invalid`/error-color state so it could visually echo its associated control's validity. Rejected because that would duplicate state that ds-form-field already owns and broadcasts through its own `error` prop and part; a label independently tracking validity risks the two falling out of sync (the label says 'invalid' while the field says 'valid') with no single source of truth. Keeping ds-label a dumb, association-only atom keeps that state in exactly one place. The `required` marker is driven by --sem-color-feedback-error (already a declared ds.contrastPair partner of --sem-color-surface-bg in tokens.json) rather than a new color, so contrast is machine-checked with zero new token surface.
